title: Loan Eligibility Basics
fullRewardHint: Answer all {{total}} choices correctly to earn the full reward.
reflectionPrompts: How can you build a strong financial profile to improve loan eligibility?
What habits should you develop early to ensure future creditworthiness?

stages.prompt: You're applying for your first loan. What factor is most important for loan approval?

options.label: Having multiple loans shows financial activity
options.reflection: Having multiple loans actually increases your debt burden and can hurt your loan eligibility by affecting your debt-to-income ratio.

options.label: Stable income and repayment discipline
options.reflection: Exactly! Lenders want to see that you have a consistent income and a track record of managing debt responsibly.

options.label: Owning expensive assets like luxury cars
options.reflection: While assets can help, lenders are more concerned with your ability to generate income and manage debt responsibly.

options.label: Having friends with good credit scores
options.reflection: Your friends' credit scores don't affect your loan eligibility - lenders focus on your individual financial behavior and capacity.

stages.prompt: Which of these demonstrates good repayment discipline to lenders?

options.label: Paying bills a few days late occasionally
options.reflection: Late payments, even by a few days, can negatively impact your credit score and loan eligibility.

options.label: Always paying only the minimum amount due
options.reflection: Paying only minimums suggests you're struggling financially and may not be able to handle additional loan payments.

options.label: Paying all bills on time, every time
options.reflection: Perfect! Consistent on-time payments demonstrate reliability and financial discipline to potential lenders.

options.label: Paying when you remember or have extra money
options.reflection: Inconsistent payment behavior signals financial instability and poor money management skills.

stages.prompt: What should you do to improve your chances of loan approval?

options.label: Spend more to show you're financially active
options.reflection: Spending more without increasing income can hurt your debt-to-income ratio and reduce your loan eligibility.

options.label: Build an emergency fund and maintain low debt
options.reflection: Excellent! Having savings shows financial responsibility, and low debt improves your debt-to-income ratio.

options.label: Borrow from multiple sources to build credit
options.reflection: Taking on multiple debts simultaneously increases your risk profile and can make you appear over-leveraged to lenders.

options.label: Ignore your credit score since it's not important
options.reflection: Your credit score is one of the most important factors lenders consider when evaluating loan applications.

stages.prompt: Why is maintaining a stable employment history important for loans?

options.label: It demonstrates reliable income generation over time
options.reflection: Correct! Stable employment shows lenders that you have a consistent source of income to repay your loan.

options.label: It shows you change jobs frequently for better opportunities
options.reflection: Frequent job changes can signal instability to lenders, making them concerned about your ability to maintain consistent income.

options.label: It helps you build professional networking connections
options.reflection: While networking is valuable, lenders are primarily concerned with your ability to generate steady income.

options.label: It proves you're gaining diverse work experience
options.reflection: Diverse experience is good, but lenders want to see job stability and consistent income rather than frequent career changes.

stages.prompt: What's the impact of having too many credit inquiries in a short period?

options.label: It increases your chances of loan approval
options.reflection: Multiple credit inquiries in a short period actually decrease your approval chances as they signal financial desperation.

options.label: It shows you're shopping for the best interest rates
options.reflection: While rate shopping is smart, too many inquiries suggest you're applying for credit frequently, which can raise red flags for lenders.

options.label: It has no effect on your financial profile
options.reflection: Credit inquiries do have an impact on your credit score and loan eligibility - lenders can see all recent inquiries on your credit report.

options.label: It can temporarily lower your credit score
options.reflection: Exactly! Each credit inquiry can slightly reduce your credit score, and multiple inquiries in a short time can significantly impact your eligibility.
